skill.name: frontend-css
skill.description: Write and maintain CSS with consistent methodology, design tokens, framework patterns, and production-ready styling practices.
skill.summary: Guide CSS, Tailwind, CSS Modules, and design-system styling work.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found parent-directory traversal text and weak-cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. The weak-cryptography findings are false positives from CSS guidance prose, while the parent-directory Markdown link is a minor filesystem-boundary concern. No prompt injection, command execution, network access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Relative Link Leaves Skill Directory
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 29 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/frontend/css.md. This is a Markdown documentation reference, not executable path handling, but it points outside the skill directory and may be non-portable or cause boundary confusion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory traversal sequence is directly present on line 29. Confidence is moderated because it appears only in a documentation link and no file-reading code is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak-cryptography matches on SKILL.md lines 3, 13, 14, and 24. These lines describe CSS, design tokens, theme configuration, and Claude Code guidance, with no cryptographic algorithm or API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain prose about frontend styling concepts only. There is no code, cryptographic library call, hash algorithm, or security-sensitive transformation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent Directory Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references a file outside its own directory with ../../../agent-os/standards/frontend/css.md. This is a low-severity portability and boundary concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relative parent path is visible in the skill text. It is not paired with executable code or instructions to exfiltrate data.

content.value_statement: Frontend projects often accumulate inconsistent styling methods and design token usage. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward consistent CSS, Tailwind, and component styling decisions.
actual_capabilities: Guides edits to CSS, SCSS, SASS, and Less files.
Supports Tailwind utility usage and framework configuration work.
Encourages consistent design tokens for color, spacing, typography, and shadows.
Helps refactor styles toward BEM, utility-first, CSS Modules, or CSS-in-JS patterns.
Covers layout, flexbox, grid, animation, transition, and override decisions.
Promotes production CSS cleanup, including unused style reduction.
limitations: The skill contains guidance only and does not include executable tooling.
A referenced standards file is outside the packaged skill directory.
It does not validate rendered UI or measure browser compatibility by itself.
It cannot replace project-specific design review or accessibility testing.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component Styling
use_cases.description: Apply consistent class names, CSS Modules, or utility patterns across reusable frontend components.

use_cases.title: Build Design Tokens
use_cases.description: Define color, spacing, typography, and shadow tokens that match an existing design system.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy CSS
use_cases.description: Convert inconsistent selectors and overrides into maintainable CSS, Tailwind, or BEM conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Simple Style Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CSS change for consistency with the project styling approach. Point out naming, spacing, and token issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small CSS edit

prompt_templates.title: Style a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Style this component using the project's existing CSS methodology. Keep layout, spacing, and typography consistent with nearby components.

prompt_templates.title: Create Design Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a practical token plan for colors, spacing, typography, and shadows based on the current UI patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor CSS Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this styling area and propose a refactor toward maintainable CSS architecture. Include migration steps and risks.

output_examples.input: A button component uses mixed inline styles, utility classes, and custom selectors.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the dominant local styling method, suggests one consistent approach, and explains token and spacing changes.

output_examples.input: A theme needs colors, spacing, and typography for a new dashboard.
output_examples.output: The response produces a concise token plan, names token groups, and explains how components should consume them.

output_examples.input: A legacy stylesheet has duplicate selectors and several override chains.
output_examples.output: The response groups the issues, recommends a staged refactor, and flags risky visual areas for regression review.

best_practices: Match the styling method already used by the surrounding code.
Use shared tokens before adding one-off colors, spacing, or shadows.
Verify layout and responsive behavior after substantial style changes.
anti_patterns: Do not mix unrelated CSS methodologies in the same component without a migration reason.
Do not add broad overrides that can affect unrelated UI areas.
Do not introduce new tokens when an existing token already fits the design.

faq.question: Does this skill write CSS code?
faq.answer: It provides guidance for AI coding tools that write or modify CSS, Tailwind, CSS Modules, and related styling code.

faq.question: Can it work with Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly covers Tailwind utility classes and framework configuration work.

faq.question: Does it support design systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides design token work for colors, spacing, typography, shadows, themes, and reusable styling rules.

faq.question: Is it limited to React projects?
faq.answer: No. It mentions React and Vue, but the guidance applies to frontend styling across many frameworks.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill file contains text guidance and no command or network instructions.

faq.question: What should publishers fix before broad distribution?
faq.answer: Package or remove the external relative standards link so all required guidance stays inside the skill directory.
